pages.raw_text:
There seems to be tendency on
the part of Commissioner ONO to
be very lenient with the Washing
ton Power Power company in the
payment for the temporary struc-
ture which has replaced the col
lapsed middle span.


When asked this afternoon if
the company had paid in check
covering the entire expense of the
structure, the commissioner said:
mot yet, but 1t would turn in q
check for about 5000, half of the
expense"


The city council distinctly Or
dered the board of public works
to go ahead and put up that tenn
porary structure at the expense of
the Washington Water Power COM
pany.


The recent change in the board.
Weymouth being out, has placed
Roy at the head of the water de-
partment and ONO in charge of
streets and bridges.


With this change in his duties
Ono has suddenly changed his
front and now contends, as Roy
did, that the Washington Water
Power company shall pay only
half.


1t looks like there's very dark
niger in somebody's wood pile.
